properties.titulo_evento: US launches new Iran strikes after two soldiers killed: What’s the latest?
properties.descricao: US strikes target Iran's IRGC after two of its soldiers were killed in Jordan, as Tehran attacks Kuwait and Bahrain.

properties.titulo_evento: Russia launches largest ballistic missile attack on Kyiv since start of war
properties.descricao: Ukraine is particularly vulnerable to ballistic missile attacks due to a shortage of Patriot air defence munitions.

properties.titulo_evento: The number one health issue in Palestine is the Israeli occupation
properties.descricao: In both Gaza and the West Bank, Israeli policies are devastating Palestinian health with deadly consequences.

properties.titulo_evento: US bombs Iran for eighth-straight night
properties.descricao: The US has launched a new wave of strikes on Iran after two American soldiers were killed by Iranian strikes in Jordan.

properties.titulo_evento: At least one killed as Russian ballistic missiles hit Kyiv
properties.descricao: Russian missiles struck Kyiv and the surrounding region early on Sunday, killing at least one person and injuring nine.

properties.titulo_evento: US and Iran exchange strikes after two US soldiers killed in Jordan
properties.descricao: Kuwait says an electricity and water plant is on fire after the second such attack in two days. Iran says US strikes hit an under-construction nuclear power plant.

properties.titulo_evento: Tate brothers arrested in US as further UK charges take total to 59
properties.descricao: The Crown Prosecution Service says it has requested their extradition from the US, while their lawyer says they are innocent.

properties.titulo_evento: Russia launches major ballistic missile attack on Ukrainian cities
properties.descricao: Four people are killed and at least 36 injured as Ukraine's two largest cities are targeted, as Kyiv continues its own assault on Russian infrastructure.

properties.titulo_evento: Cuban dissident Luis Manuel Otero Alcántara goes into exile in US
properties.descricao: He was arrested in 2021 during Cuba's largest anti-government protests in decades and spent five years ⁠in prison.

properties.titulo_evento: White House defends Argentina team over Falklands banner
properties.descricao: The comments could further fuel the row over the incident, which has seen Downing Street back calls for Fifa to investigate.

properties.titulo_evento: One anti-war critic fined, another held as Russia clamps down on dissent
properties.descricao: While Boris Nadezhdin is barred from running for parliament, blogger Ilya Remeslo is remanded in custody.

properties.titulo_evento: Russians turn to cash, putting more strain on slowing wartime economy
properties.descricao: Russians have been hit by mobile internet shutdowns and more businesses are seeking to dodge tax after more than four years of war with Ukraine.

properties.titulo_evento: Andrew and Tristan Tate arrested in Miami as UK seeks extradition
properties.descricao: Influencer brothers Andrew and Tristan Tate have been arrested in Miami ahead of an extradition request from the United Kingdom. British authorities accuse them of serious offences, including rape, human trafficking and sexual exploitation.

properties.titulo_evento: Trump queries Tuchel's tactics in England's semifinal heartbreak against Argentina
properties.descricao: "They took their best player, and they put him on defence," Trump said, referring to England striker Harry Kane.

properties.titulo_evento: US forces strike Iran for seventh consecutive night
properties.descricao: Iran's Fars state news agency reported that Iranian forces had responded by attacking US allies and bases in the region, including the Ali Al Salem airbase in Kuwait.

properties.titulo_evento: EU foreign policy chief Kallas visits EU naval mission in the Red Sea
properties.descricao: EU foreign policy chief Kaja Kallas visited personnel serving with the EU's ASPIDES and ATALANTA naval missions, receiving operational briefings and meeting troops deployed to help protect shipping routes in the Red Sea. Euronews correspondent Toby G...

properties.titulo_evento: 7.3 magnitude earthquake strikes Mexico and Guatemala coasts, tsunami alert lifted
properties.descricao: A 7.3 magnitude earthquake shook the coasts of Mexico and Guatemala on Friday, its epicentre off Chiapas at about 15 km deep.

properties.titulo_evento: China rejects Trump's election interference accusations as 'groundless'
properties.descricao: At a daily briefing in Beijing, a spokesperson called on the US to stop making what it described as 'groundless accusations' against China.

properties.titulo_evento: China rescuers race to find trapped people after landslide
properties.descricao: China launched a large-scale rescue operation after a landslide struck Pengshui County in Chongqing municipality on 17 July, burying part of a residential area and trapping an unknown number of people.

properties.titulo_evento: England stun France in goal fest for third place
properties.descricao: England finish third after dominating in the first half. France managed to claw its way back into the game, but did not manage to find the win for Didier Deschamps' last match as French manager. Mbappé becomes the highest scoring player in world cup...

properties.titulo_evento: The week in pictures: The end of the World Cup, heatwaves and wildfires, war in Ukraine
properties.descricao: The final stages of the World Cup, with Les Bleus’ elimination and England finishing third; extreme heat and wildfires around the world; and the Tour de France brought to a standstill by the heatwave in France. FRANCE 24 looks back at some of the we...

properties.titulo_evento: Russia launches deadly strikes on Kyiv after attack on Moscow warehouses
properties.descricao: Russia fired two dozen ballistic missiles at Kyiv overnight on Sunday, killing one person and wounding 16, in an ever-escalating air war that is taking an increasing civilian toll on both sides. The latest with France 24 correspondent Emmanuelle Cha...

properties.titulo_evento: The Bayeux tapestry arrives in London for display at British Museum
properties.descricao: The thousand-year-old, 70-metre-long Bayeux Tapestry, which depicts William the Conqueror's campaign in medieval England, has been carefully removed from its transport crate following its transfer from France. Conservators will begin a detailed cond...

properties.titulo_evento: Ukraine: Thousands take to the streets to protest against Fedorov removal
properties.descricao: Protests continued across Ukraine for a third consecutive day despite the ongoing war, with demonstrators demanding the reinstatement of the popular former defence minister, Mykhailo Fedorov, following his removal. The demonstrations coincided with ...

properties.titulo_evento: Russia's largest ballistic assault of war rocks Kyiv
properties.descricao: Fires broke out across the Ukrainian capital after Russia's latest missile attack, which used the highest number of ballistic missiles of the war so far. Several people have been injured in Kyiv.

properties.titulo_evento: Andrew Tate, brother arrested in Miami, UK seeks extradition
properties.descricao: Andrew and Tristan Tate were taken into custody by the US Marshals Service in Miami on a sealed warrant. The UK has requested their extradition on charges that include trafficking and rape.

properties.titulo_evento: US strikes Iran after 2 service members killed in Jordan
properties.descricao: The US military struck Iran after two US soldiers were reported killed in an Iranian attack on a US base in Jordan. Meanwhile, Kuwait and Bahrain reported fresh Iranian attacks. DW has the latest.

properties.titulo_evento: Ukraine drones target Russian warehouses and oil sites
properties.descricao: Ukrainian drones have struck warehouses, an oil depot and other targets across Russia, killing nine people and injuring over 80 more. Kyiv said its strikes targeted suppliers for Moscow's ongoing invasion of Ukraine.

properties.titulo_evento: Germany news: Minister warns of high terror threat
properties.descricao: Germany's interior minister has warned of a heightened security threat, saying an attack could be imminent. Meanwhile, a train security guard was critically injured after falling from a moving train. DW has the latest.

properties.titulo_evento: Russia hits Ukraine with one of the largest ballistic barrages of the war
properties.descricao: The overnight strikes highlighted Ukraine's shortage of air defenses, as Kyiv pressed allies for more interceptors and the EU for stalled sanctions against Moscow.

properties.titulo_evento: Die Arktis im Fokus und Putin unter Druck. Mit Florence Gaub

properties.titulo_evento: Wie sich Europa für den Ernstfall vorbereitet. Mit NATO-General Laubenthal
properties.descricao: Der NATO-Gipfel in Ankara hat der Allianz wichtige Entscheidungen für neue Aufklärungssysteme gebracht. Im Interview mit Gordon Repinski blickt Bundeswehr-General Markus Laubenthal, Chief of Staff im Supreme Headquarters Allied Powers Europe, gelasse...

properties.titulo_evento: Trump threatens Canada over wildfire smoke choking US cities
properties.descricao: Soot from Canadian wildfires blanketed a dozen U.S. states Friday, as government agencies warned millions of Americans to stay indoors.

properties.titulo_evento: Russia Bill Broadens Sanctions but Could Fuel Fears Over the Dollar
properties.descricao: The Trump administration has recently sought to scale back sanctions as adversaries develop workarounds that some believe could erode the U.S. dollar’s supremacy.

properties.titulo_evento: Pentagon’s Escort Policy for Journalists Is Temporarily Restored
properties.descricao: An appeals court said the Defense Department could require escorts for reporters who visit the Pentagon while The New York Times sues to overturn the rule.

properties.titulo_evento: Stocks Sink on Anxiety About Tech and A.I. Spending
properties.descricao: Investors are growing uneasy about increasing competition from China in the global race to dominate artificial intelligence.
